name: Chain Sling Working Load Limit Calculator — EN 818 / ASME B30 Chain Sling Utilization Check
subtitle: Lifting / Rigging / Chain Slings — EN 818-4, EN 818-5, EN 818-6, ASME B30.9, ASME B30.26, ASME P30.1, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1

scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Chain Sling Rating Standard
inputs.hint: Applicable standard for chain sling rating. EN 818-4: Grade 8 chain slings. ASME B30.9: includes chain slings in Table 3. ISO 1835: older standard (increasingly replaced by EN 818).

inputs.name: Chain Grade / Quality
inputs.hint: Chain material grade. Grade 8 (EN 818 / ASME B30.9): most common for overhead lifting. Grade 10/12: higher strength (less common). Grade 5: tie-down/towing (not for overhead lifting per ASME B30.9). Grade 100/120: newer high-strength grades.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Chain grade marking — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Nominal Chain Diameter
inputs.hint: Nominal diameter of chain link material. EN 818-4: 4–45 mm. ASME B30.9: up to 32 mm typical. Measure with caliper across the link cross-section.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Precision measurement tolerance: ±0.2% for calibrated measurement

inputs.name: Single Leg WLL (Vertical, per chain sling leg)
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of a single chain sling leg in vertical configuration per EN 818-4 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Grade 8, 10mm chain: ~31.5 kN. Verify from manufacturer tag.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §6.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes proof test variability)

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load including all attachments and rigging hardware suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% for weighed loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Use local value for precision critical lifts.

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor for dynamic effects: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for shock loading. EN 818-4: 1.0 for static proof load, 1.25 for normal service.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §7.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts

inputs.name: Chain Sling Configuration
inputs.hint: Configuration of the chain sling assembly. EN 818-5 §5 defines assembly types. Bridle = multiple legs from master link. Basket = chain wraps around load. Choker = chain passes through itself.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §5 — Configuration definitions, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Installed Sling Legs
inputs.hint: Total number of chain legs in the sling assembly. ASME B30.9: maximum 4 legs. EN 818-4: single, 2-leg, 3-leg, 4-leg assemblies defined.

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg or 4-leg configurations without load-balancing: often only 2 legs bear the load. EN 818-5 §6.2: if load equalizing not ensured, use reduced leg count.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.2 — Load distribution assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, compensation ring) used to ensure equal load distribution among all legs?

inputs.name: Angle from Vertical (β)
inputs.hint: Angle between the chain sling leg and the vertical axis. EN 818-5: maximum 45° for rated configurations (reduces to 0° vertical). 0° = vertical, 30° = 1.155x factor, 45° = 1.414x factor, 60° = 2.0x factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° field measurement

inputs.name: Rated Configuration Factor (C_rated)
inputs.hint: Configuration factor from EN 818-5 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Single vertical: 1.0. 2-leg bridle: 1.0 per leg. Basket (choked): 0.8. Basket (full): 2.0. Choker: 0.75. Verify from sling tag if leg-specific WLL is marked.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 Table 1 — Configuration factors, zero measurement uncertainty for standard configurations

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor for unequal load distribution. 1.0 = verified equal distribution (load-balancing device). 0.9 = good distribution (symmetric geometry, rigid load). 0.8 = moderate distribution. 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs).

inputs.name: Operating Temperature
inputs.hint: Ambient/operating temperature at the chain sling during the lift. EN 818-4 §7.3: Grade 8 rated for -40°C to +200°C standard. Above 200°C: capacity reduction required. Above 300°C: special alloy chain required.

inputs.name: Manufacturer Temperature Limit
inputs.hint: Maximum operating temperature specified by the chain sling manufacturer. Grade 8 standard: 200°C. Extended temperature range chains may be rated to 300°C or higher. Verify from sling tag or manufacturer data sheet.

inputs.name: Temperature Capacity Factor (f_temp)
inputs.hint: Capacity derating factor for elevated temperature. EN 818-4 §7.3: T ≤ 200°C → 1.0; 200°C < T ≤ 300°C → 0.8; 300°C < T ≤ 400°C → 0.5. Below -40°C → use cold-temperature rated chain only.

inputs.name: Edge Contact / Bend Radius Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact or sharp bend radius on chain links. 1.0 = no edge contact, proper radius. 0.9 = rounded edges with padding. 0.8 = soft metal edge contact. 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. EN 818-6 §8.5: sharp edges must be protected.

inputs.name: Wear Derating Factor (f_wear)
inputs.hint: Derating for chain link wear (reduction in link diameter). ASME B30.9 §6.2.3: reject chain when wear exceeds 10% of original diameter. f_wear = (actual_diameter / original_diameter). 1.0 = no significant wear; 0.9 = 10% wear (rejection limit); 0.5 = 50% section loss.

inputs.name: Additional Derating Factor (f_add)
inputs.hint: Additional derating factor for any other conditions affecting capacity (corrosion, link deformation, nicks/gouges, fatigue cracks, or adverse environmental conditions not covered by other factors). Default 1.0 = no additional derating.

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly: master links, intermediate links, hooks, shackles, rings, or load pins. ASME B30.26: all components must be rated and marked with WLL.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, chain size, serial number, manufacturer ID, and standard reference) present and legible on the chain sling assembly per EN 818-6 §7 or ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?

inputs.name: Manufacturer Certificate Available
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer test certificate (EN 818-4 §8: proof load test, material traceability, batch number) available and traceable?

inputs.name: Chain Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Inspection status per EN 818-6 §6 or ASME B30.9 §6. PASS = no defects. CONDITIONAL = minor issues requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-6 §6 — Inspection status, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Chain Twisted or Knotted
inputs.hint: Is the chain sling twisted or knotted? EN 818-6 §8.2: chain slings must not be used if twisted, knotted, or kinked. Twisted chains have reduced capacity and may fail unexpectedly.

inputs.name: Chemical Exposure Status
inputs.hint: Exposure to chemicals that may affect chain integrity. EN 818-6 §8.6: chain slings must not be exposed to corrosive chemicals without protective coating. Acid/alkali exposure requires immediate inspection and possible replacement.

inputs.name: Personnel Lifting Application
inputs.hint: Is this chain sling used for personnel lifting? EN 818-6 §4: chain slings for personnel lifting require 10:1 design factor (vs. 4:1 for material lifting) and additional inspection/certification requirements.

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated: ±5%. Estimated: ±10%.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in angle from vertical measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.

inputs.name: Chain Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in chain sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated (certified): ±3%. Used/aged sling (wear not quantified): ±5–10%.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: angle out of range 0–60° (V1)
fmea.effect: Angle exceeds EN 818-5 limit — excessive leg tension
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: active leg count invalid (V2)
fmea.effect: Invalid leg count for load distribution
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Load weight undefined — lift not possible
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Non-conservative dynamic factor — may underestimate load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: no load-balancing with ≥3 legs (V5)
fmea.effect: Unequal load distribution — conservative assessment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic WARNING on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Chain sling condemned — do not use
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chain twisted or knotted (V11)
fmea.effect: EN 818-6 §8.2 violation — reduced capacity, unpredictable failure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: personnel lifting without EN 818 (V13)
fmea.effect: Inadequate design factor for personnel safety
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: personnel lifting with non-PASS inspection (V14)
fmea.effect: Inadequate inspection status for personnel lifting
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Tension exceeds WLL — potential chain failure and dropped load
fmea.control_measure: EN 818-5 §6.6 — UC threshold monitoring with critical alert

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ANGLE: β > 45° for chain sling
fmea.effect: Exceeds EN 818-5 rated angle range for standard assemblies
Reduced chain strength at elevated temperature
Reduced cross-section from wear — reduced capacity
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9 §6.2.3 — Wear measurement and rejection criteria
